description: Trigger discrimination: the traffic/scenario PLAN (this skill) vs the measuring instrument (performance-test-harness, the sibling), target setting (slo-reliability-architect), test-data generation (test-data-architect), and overall QA strategy (qa-strategy-architect).

cases.prompt: Plan the load for our pre-release validation: realistic endpoint mix with the right write share, our two whale tenants plus the long tail, a soak overnight, and a spike scenario for launch morning — with abort criteria.
cases.reason: Workload realism, tenant mix, soak/spike scenario selection, and abort profiles are the traffic plan's anatomy. No measurement mechanics, no strategy-level layer decisions, no target setting requested.

cases.prompt: Design how results get judged run over run: environment pinning, baseline storage, repeat-run noise bands, and which gates block a release in CI.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (sibling, reverse direction) — judging results (baselines, noise bands, gates, environment contracts) is the INSTRUMENT. The planner supplies what load runs; everything in this ask is about how measurement executes and decides.
cases.reason: Baseline lifecycle and regression gating are harness design; no scenario or workload content appears.

cases.prompt: What latency and availability should we actually be promising at peak, and how much error budget do we have to spend on launches?
cases.reason: The promises and error budgets are the SLO catalog's to set. The load plan CITES those numbers as pass/fail; a plan asked to invent them hits its own stop condition and routes here.

cases.prompt: Generate the seed data for the perf environment: a few million synthetic orders shaped like production including the big customer's skew, PII-safe, rebuildable between runs.
cases.note: SEAM CASE — the plan STATES volumes and shapes it needs; generating them (synthetic data, PII-safe paths, rebuild determinism) is test-data-architect's whole discipline.
cases.reason: Everything requested is data-generation mechanics; the load plan is upstream demand, not the generator.

cases.prompt: Define our overall testing strategy — what gets unit, integration, E2E, and manual coverage, who owns each layer, and what evidence gates a release.
cases.reason: Product-level layer split and evidence-per-change-class is QA strategy. Performance/load validation is one slice that strategy may commission — the planner enters only when a load scenario is actually being designed.
